text:
The Underground Clinic Nurse is the head nurse of the Underground Clinic in the Republic of East Gorteau. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 255 

Appearance

The Underground Clinic Nurse is a short, black haired, narrow eyed, and stout looking lady, dressed in standard nurse clothing with a stethoscope hung around her neck. 

Personality

The nurse is shown to be extremely greedy since she is willing to charge medical fees at cutthroat prices for every patient in the clinic. She is also very forceful to the point of sadism in which she refuses to let any patient leave the clinic unless they pay their ridiculously huge medical fees or else she will take their body parts as payment. Her greediness also extends to other services like phone bills since she charges Killua 10,000 per minute. 

Plot

Chimera Ant arc

The Underground Clinic Nurse approaches Killua and Ikalgo as soon as Killua awakens from his two day coma. She informs them that if Killua happened to die, he wouldn't need a grave as his body would have been harvested and sold. When Killua says he's ready to leave the clinic, the Nurse tells him that he can't leave unless he pays for his medical fees that added up to 1,800,000 and if he can't pay, the clinic can accept a body part instead. Killua bluffs that he left his wallet at home and asks if he can leave to go get it, but the Nurse refuses saying that he'll have to stay until he finds a way to pay for his fees. Killua, irritated by the nurse's unwillingness to cooperate with him, then asks to use the phone. The Nurse informs him it's 10,000 per minute and because Pail is the currency used at the clinic, she can exchange 500,000 for one Pail. Livid by the Nurse's greed, Killua tells the Nurse to buzz off and uses the phone to contact Gon, while the Nurse pesters him for money. Finished with his phone call, Killua gives the Nurse his account number to his bank and after it clears, she allows him to leave. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

It is possible that the nurse has ties with the Mafia community due to the nature of her clinic.
